title: nuphar pumila subsp. sinensis
intro: Nuphar pumila subsp. sinensis, also known as the Chinese water lily or Oriental water lily, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the Nuphar genus. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Nuphar pumila subsp. sinensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even treat certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its rhizomes and roots, which are dried and then used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
